authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 396 - Inspection, Repair, and Maintenance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 396 is the primary U.S. public regulatory baseline for commercial fleet inspection, maintenance, DVIR, periodic inspection, record retention, and unsafe-operation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: maintenance intake, defect review, repair execution, periodic inspection, and completion recordkeeping
used_for: authoritative maintenance and inspection records posture

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 565 - Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 565 defines the VIN structure used to anchor vehicle identity, plant information, and recall lookups in automotive fleet workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: asset identity validation and VIN-linked record reconciliation

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 573 - Defect and Noncompliance Responsibility and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 573 is the primary U.S. public rule for defect and recall reporting, risk-to-safety descriptions, chronology, and remedy-program expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall, defect, and noncompliance hold or remedy escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Check for Recalls: Vehicle, Car Seat, Tire, Equipment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's official recalls workflow explains VIN-specific recall lookup behavior, limitations, manufacturer remedy expectations, and the agency's recall-management role.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: VIN lookup, recall verification, hold, and return-to-service review

authoritative_sources.title: VIN Decoder
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's VIN decoder is the official public operating reference for decoding VIN-linked plant and vehicle identity details used in fleet record reconciliation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: asset identity, manufacturer, plant, and country-of-origin validation
used_for: record reconciliation when unit metadata conflicts

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 55001:2024 - Asset management - Asset management system - Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 55001 is the recognized asset-management standard for controlling asset lifecycle, balancing performance, risk, and cost, and maintaining a systematic asset-management framework.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lifecycle-state control, risk-aware maintenance governance, and return-to-service discipline

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for interoperable traceability, and its traceability standard defines the CTE and KDE framework relevant to fleet parts and supplier evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-enterprise parts movement, receipt, issue, and supplier evidence exchange
used_for: cross-system parts and supplier traceability discipline

authoritative_sources.title: EPCIS Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 EPCIS is the recognized public standard for sharing visibility event data across enterprises, which is directly useful for part movement, custody, and maintenance-event traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: parts issue, movement, fitment, removal, and return-event recording

authoritative_sources.title: Traceability of Vehicle Components
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Odette is a recognized automotive supply-chain standards body, and this recommendation is a directly automotive-specific public reference for component traceability, supplier/customer reference exchange, and claim handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: part marking, supplier and customer reference exchange, claim handling, and component traceability

authoritative_sources.title: CVSA's 2026 Out-of-Service Criteria Now in Effect
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CVSA's annual out-of-service criteria are the recognized North American operating reference enforcement personnel use with federal regulations to decide whether drivers or vehicles present an imminent hazard and should be placed out of service.

workflow_stages.name: asset identity and intake validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the correct unit, VIN, odometer or engine-hour state, work-order context, and asset class before any maintenance-state recommendation is made.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: maintenance trigger, inspection, and downtime triage
workflow_stages.description: Review DVIRs, roadside inspection reports, PM schedules, telematics faults, and reported defects to decide whether the work is ready, blocked, unsafe, or on hold.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: parts, supplier, and traceability reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile issued parts, fitment references, lot or serial details, supplier information, and storeroom or warehouse evidence before repair or return-to-service progression.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: repair, rework, recall, and out-of-service exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Manage failed verification, unsafe-operation findings, recall holds, supplier or alternate-part pressure, and other blocked states while preserving formal approval ownership.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and return-to-service packaging
workflow_stages.description: Close fleet work only when inspection, repair, traceability, and signoff evidence is present, and escalate licensed, safety-critical, recall, or supplier-approval decisions instead of absorbing them.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: unit identifiers, VIN records, registration-linked vehicle identity, and asset metadata
maintenance work orders, PM schedules, repair orders, technician notes, and closure checklists
DVIRs, roadside inspection reports, annual or periodic inspection reports, and defect clearance evidence
parts reservations, issue and return transactions, fitment records, lot or serial references, and supplier records
telematics fault codes, diagnostics, outage reason codes, and readiness or downtime states
quality holds, nonconformance reports, recall or campaign status, warranty records, and change-control documentation
road-test or post-repair verification records, return-to-service signoffs, and escalation notes

allowed: classify fleet work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework-pending, return-to-service ready, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, VIN and unit context, inspection records, parts traceability, and systems-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: licensed or qualified-inspector signoff, safety-critical release, or legal authorization to operate a vehicle
final engineering, warranty, recall remedy certification, or policy-override decisions
supplier approval, alternate-part approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
quality disposition, procurement authorization, or logistics release when those owners control the decision
refuse: continue or complete work when VIN, inspection, defect, recall, or parts traceability evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark fleet work complete without closure checklist, defect clearance, inspection, or return-to-service evidence
bypass out-of-service, recall, quality, or supplier controls to recover uptime

domain_failure_modes: the wrong unit or VIN is serviced because asset identity records were not reconciled
a vehicle returns to service despite an open recall, unsafe-operation finding, or missing qualified inspection evidence
CMMS, ERP, WMS, and parts fitment records conflict on which component was installed or removed
supplier substitutions or alternate parts are treated as routine uptime decisions instead of controlled approvals
fleet work is declared complete without defect clearance, road-test, inspection, or closure evidence
warranty claims, field reports, and defect chronology are ignored when safety-related nonconformance is present
the lane drifts into final safety, engineering, legal, warranty, or supplier-approval authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific inspection programs beyond FMCSA baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 10, 2026, FMCSA Part 396 is the public baseline for commercial motor vehicles in interstate commerce, but tenants operating light-duty fleets, intrastate fleets, or jurisdiction-specific inspection programs must supply the governing local inspection and release rules before the lane treats those local rules as controlling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific approval chain for alternate parts, EV high-voltage release, and warranty exceptions
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources establish maintenance, recall, and traceability boundaries, but they do not define a universal approval chain for alternate parts, EV high-voltage return-to-service, or warranty overrides. Tenant-approved supplier lists, engineering rules, and safety signoff matrices remain mandatory runtime inputs.

notes: Authoritative fleet records are the tenant's designated CMMS, ERP, telematics, inventory, supplier, inspection, and recall records, constrained by FMCSA, NHTSA, ISO, GS1, Odette, and CVSA baselines where applicable.

The lane stops before final safety release, licensed inspection signoff, engineering disposition, warranty adjudication, supplier approval, and legal or regulatory interpretation even when uptime pressure is high.
